methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Space & Aerospace (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Space & Aerospace, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 25.8x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
Bellatrix Aerospace is a pre-Series B stage space and aerospace company that has raised $31M to date. The valuation universe comprises 5 public comps (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private peers including SpaceX, Sierra Space, Planet Labs, Impulse Space, and Apex Space.

Standard triangulation methods are not applicable here. Peer-median analysis fails due to insufficient narrow-stage cohort—while 11 same-sector peers exist, none meet the minimum threshold of three datapoints when filtered by stage, adjacency, or valuation tier. Public-comp anchoring cannot proceed because the target's revenue is not on file; though the Space & Aerospace sector median trades at 25.8x P/S across the six public comps identified, this multiple cannot be applied without a disclosed revenue figure for Bellatrix.

The analysis therefore cannot support a quantitative valuation range at this time. The key uncertainties are twofold: first, the absence of a sufficiently dense peer set at the pre-Series B stage in space and aerospace limits comparable company inference; second, lack of revenue disclosure prevents application of sector multiples. Any valuation must await either disclosure of Bellatrix's revenue, identification of additional same-stage private comparables with known metrics, or reliance on alternative methods such as discounted cash flow if forward projections are available.
